verdict.answer_first: A and B both answer in sentence 1; A is slightly more immediate with 'three lines' after 'flexbox on the parent', while B adds 'cleanest modern way' before the code.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits a bit harder: 'For almost all cases, reach for flexbox first'; B also says 'For most cases, reach for Flexbox' but spends more space surveying options.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it includes the needed vertical-height caveat: 'the parent needs a height' plus a complete HTML example.
